is a 3.1 kilometre street in [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney"), Australia running from [Victoria Road](/wiki/Victoria_Road,_Sydney "Victoria Road, Sydney") to [Balmain East ferry wharf](/wiki/Balmain_East_ferry_wharf "Balmain East ferry wharf"). It is the main thoroughfare and high street of the suburbs of [Rozelle](/wiki/Rozelle "Rozelle") and [Balmain](/wiki/Balmain,_New_South_Wales "Balmain, New South Wales"), and a noted café and restaurant strip.

Hochuli, Daniel (3 December 2009). ["Daniel Hochuli's Balmain Grind: The Origins of Darling Street"](https://balmaingrind.blogspot.com/2009/12/origins-of-darling-street.html). *Daniel Hochuli's Balmain Grind*. Retrieved 16 June 2024.
The Balmain Counterweight System - 1903 to 1955 *[Electric Traction](/wiki/Transit_Australia "Transit Australia")* November 1961 pages 10-13

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Street in Sydney, Australia

| Darling Street   [New South Wales](/wiki/New_South_Wales "New South Wales") | |
| --- | --- |
| General information | |
| Type | Street |
| Length | 3.1 km (1.9 mi) |
| Major junctions | |
| West end | [Victoria Road](/wiki/Victoria_Road,_Sydney "Victoria Road, Sydney") |
| East end | [Balmain East ferry wharf](/wiki/Balmain_East_ferry_wharf "Balmain East ferry wharf") |
| Location(s) | |
| Suburb(s) | [Rozelle](/wiki/Rozelle "Rozelle"), [Balmain](/wiki/Balmain,_New_South_Wales "Balmain, New South Wales") |

**Darling Street** is a 3.1 kilometre street in [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney"), Australia running from [Victoria Road](/wiki/Victoria_Road,_Sydney "Victoria Road, Sydney") to [Balmain East ferry wharf](/wiki/Balmain_East_ferry_wharf "Balmain East ferry wharf"). It is the main thoroughfare and high street of the suburbs of [Rozelle](/wiki/Rozelle "Rozelle") and [Balmain](/wiki/Balmain,_New_South_Wales "Balmain, New South Wales"), and a noted café and restaurant strip.

Balmain Market is on the corner of Darling and Curtis Road.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darling_Street&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **does not [cite](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") any [sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this section](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Darling_Street "Special:EditPage/Darling Street") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and [removed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Burden_of_evidence "Wikipedia:Verifiability"). *(March 2023)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5d/RozelleSchool.JPG/250px-RozelleSchool.JPG)](/wiki/File:RozelleSchool.JPG)

Rozelle Public School, designed by [John Horbury Hunt](/wiki/John_Horbury_Hunt "John Horbury Hunt")

Darling Street was named after General Sir [Ralph Darling](/wiki/Ralph_Darling "Ralph Darling"), a British Army officer who served as Governor of New South Wales from 1825 to 1831.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In 1875, Darling Street received its first installation of gas lighting. The effect of this was so that during the late 1880s there was an increase of business due to the increase of illumination along the shopping strip at night. Twenty years prior to this the area where the town hall is now located was occupied by bushland.

## Trams

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darling_Street&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Trams")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d8/Rozelletram.jpg/250px-Rozelletram.jpg)](/wiki/File:Rozelletram.jpg)

[Tram 2010](/wiki/Sydney_R1-Class_Tram "Sydney R1-Class Tram") at the [Darling Street wharf](/wiki/Balmain_East_ferry_wharf "Balmain East ferry wharf")

Main article: [Trams in Sydney](/wiki/Trams_in_Sydney "Trams in Sydney")

Trams once ran all the way down Darling Street to [Balmain East wharf](/wiki/Balmain_East_ferry_wharf "Balmain East ferry wharf"). The first steam tram to service Darling Street was in 1892. Ten years later the service was electrified.[[3]](#cite_note-3) A feature of this line was a counterweight dummy system that controlled and assisted trams on a steep single-line section of track near the terminus at Darling Wharf. Due to the very steep (1 in 8) descent at the eastern end of the street beyond Nicholson Street, the trams used a complex counterweight system constructed under the road surface. The trams were pushed up the steep hill by the dummy, and rode the dummy on the way down to safely descend the hill.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Gladstone Park Reservoir

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Darling_Street&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Gladstone Park Reservoir")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **does not [cite](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources "Wikipedia:Citing sources") any [sources](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this section](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Darling_Street "Special:EditPage/Darling Street") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and [removed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability#Burden_of_evidence "Wikipedia:Verifiability"). *(March 2023)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Construction of the Gladstone Park Reservoir was announced in 1912 causing public outcry. The water board reassured residents that the park and bandstand would be restored to its former status once the reservoir was complete. Before construction of the reservoir residents obtained their water supply from backyard wells or water carts. Completed in 1917 the reservoir is still intact and the water is now used for reserve supply only.
